title: ADR 0006 ‚Äî getFiscalPeriod standalone ledger date utility scoped & handed off
content: Scoped (TEAM HANDOFF, seed sdlc2/ai-bookkeeper-cert-5) a single 3-pt task for Dev Clone: add src/features/ledger/lib/period.ts exporting getFiscalPeriod(date, fiscalYearStartMonth=1) -> {year, quarter 1|2|3|4, month(fiscal 1-12), periodStart, periodEnd}, plus period.test.ts and a barrel export in index.ts. Pinned two ambiguities the spec left open: (1) 'period' unit = the CALENDAR MONTH containing the date (ERP/bookkeeping accounting-period convention) so periodStart/periodEnd bound that month; (2) 'year' = the calendar year in which the fiscal year STARTS. fiscalMonth=((calMonth-fyStart+12)%12)+1, quarter=floor((fiscalMonth-1)/3)+1. Use LOCAL date accessors + numeric Date constructor in tests for TZ-determinism. Fail loud with RangeError (matches round/arithmetic/format/amount sibling-lib convention). Key team conventions reconfirmed: tests run on Node built-in runner via tsx (node:test + node:assert/strict), NOT vitest; validation = npm run typecheck / npm test / npm run lint. This utility has ZERO money semantics so it does not interact with ADR 0001-0005. Strict scope boundary enforced for Release Manager: repo.patch must contain ONLY the 3 source files + CHANGELOG/version-bump/release-notes.

title: Session capture (Stop) ‚Äî 2026-05-28 23:15 UTC
ADR-0006 getFiscalPeriod: implement-from-scratch handoff, committed 44191ce
content: ai-bookkeeper team-mode handoff (seed sdlc2/ai-bookkeeper-cert-5). Like ADR-0006 trial-balance, this was a genuine implement-from-scratch (NOT verify+commit): git status was clean on main with no period.ts present ‚Äî the Architect handed a spec-only work order, not on-disk code (tell: source_output outcome_assessment said 'I deliberately did not write the implementation'). Built src/features/ledger/lib/period.ts exporting getFiscalPeriod(date, fiscalYearStartMonth=1): FiscalPeriod {year, quarter 1-4, month (fiscal 1-12), periodStart, periodEnd}, pure/side-effect-free per the co-located lib pattern, fail-loud RangeError (non-finite date OR fiscalYearStartMonth outside integer 1..12, so 0 and 13 throw). Pinned conventions: period unit = calendar month containing date (ERP accounting-period), year = calendar year the fiscal year STARTS, local date accessors + numeric Date constructor for TZ-determinism. fiscalMonth=((calMonth-fyStart+12)%12)+1, quarter=floor((fiscalMonth-1)/3)+1, periodEnd via new Date(y, calMonth, 0, 23,59,59,999). Added period.test.ts (10 cases incl leap Feb29, contiguous boundaries, invalid fyStart) + barrel export in index.ts. Gate: typecheck 0, npm test 87/87 (10 new), eslint clean. Committed ONLY the 3 scoped files as 44191ce on main, unpushed (review_each_pr ‚Üí human merge gate); handed to Code Reviewer via implementation.completed. The src/app/page.tsx no-html-link-for-pages eslint error that gated ADR-0004 stays resolved ‚Äî eslint . clean for a 2nd consecutive run. Obsidian vault tools were unavailable this run.

title: Session capture (Stop) ‚Äî 2026-05-28 23:17 UTC
ADR-0006 getFiscalPeriod reviewed APPROVE (44191ce) ‚Äî clean date util, build-integrity gate passed
content: Reviewed commit 44191ce (ADR-0006: getFiscalPeriod in src/features/ledger/lib/period.ts) on ai-bookkeeper. Verdict APPROVE ‚Äî no security/correctness/critical blockers; review completed in full (not blocked). Pure side-effect-free date utility, no money semantics, so it does NOT inherit the half-up-vs-half-even gap flagged in ADR-0003 arithmetic. Math re-derived by hand against all 4 ADR worked examples and confirmed: fiscalMonth=((calMonth-fyStart+12)%12)+1, quarter=floor((fiscalMonth-1)/3)+1 (the `as 1|2|3|4` cast is sound since fiscalMonth‚àà1..12), year-label=calMonth>=fyStart?calYear:calYear-1, periodEnd via new Date(calYear,calMonth,0,...) correctly gives leap Feb29 and 1ms-contiguous non-overlapping month boundaries. Fail-loud RangeError on non-finite/non-Date date and fiscalYearStartMonth outside integer 1..12 (0 and 13 rejected). Build-integrity gate PASSED: git ls-files confirms ALL barrel siblings (amount/round/arithmetic/format/period/trial-balance/ingest/data/types) are tracked + clean tree ‚Äî no untracked-sibling trap like ADR-0002's b29eeaa. Gate re-run independently green: typecheck 0, npm test 87/87 (10 new), eslint . clean (page.tsx no-html-link error stays resolved for a 3rd consecutive ledger run). Low non-blocking: ingest.ts is the only lib without a co-located test (pre-existing, out of scope). Commit unpushed on main under review_each_pr ‚Äî APPROVE clears the human merge gate. Obsidian vault tools unavailable this run. Reviewer value-add pattern holds: re-run gate + barrel-tracking check + hand-verify the new math, not re-implement.

title: Session capture (Stop) ‚Äî 2026-05-28 23:20 UTC
ai-bookkeeper @ 44191ce: FIRST API route /api/categorize adds prompt-injection + cost-DoS findings; postcss CVE still open (5th scan); generative-ai dep now USED (prior Low resolved)
content:
Re-scan at HEAD 44191ce. BIG CHANGE: src/app/api/categorize/route.ts is the FIRST server API route (prior memory said 'no app/api routes' ‚Äî now stale). It POSTs to Gemini gemini-3.5-flash via dynamic import of @google/generative-ai (route.ts:48-49), with heuristic fallback in src/lib/categorize.ts.

NEW FINDINGS:
- M1 (Medium): POST /api/categorize (route.ts:61) is UNAUTHENTICATED + UNTHROTTLED and calls the paid Gemini model per request ‚Üí cost/billing DoS. Fix: auth + rate-limit + call budget before generateContent (route.ts:51).
- M2 (Medium): prompt injection ‚Äî user description interpolated raw into prompt at src/lib/categorize.ts:215 (buildPrompt, `Description: ${req.description}`). Bounded by parseModelJson shape-validation + heuristic fallback + React JSX escaping ({result.rationale}), so Medium not High, but feeds financial categorization ‚Üí not downgraded further. Fix: delimit user input as untrusted + validate returned accounts against chart-of-accounts allow-list.
- L1 (Low): no length cap on description / no body-size limit (route.ts:22 parseRequest) ‚Üí token-cost/memory, compounds M1.
- L2 (Low): parseModelJson (categorize.ts:199) returns unbounded model-controlled strings to client+ledger; safe in React today, unescaped in any non-React sink.

RESOLVED: @google/generative-ai ^0.24.1 was flagged unused in 12bc223/b510ec9 scans ‚Äî now actually imported in route.ts ‚Üí prior Low CLOSED.

STILL OPEN: H1 postcss XSS GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (<8.5.10), 5th consecutive scan. No overrides block in package.json; next@16.2.6 nests postcss@8.4.31 (package-lock.json:3485); npm audit=2 moderate (postcss + next.via=[postcss]). Fix = one-line overrides {postcss:'>=8.5.10'}; npm's next@9.3.3 suggestion is wrong breaking downgrade. High per known-CVE policy, reachability low (build-time CSS stringify).

CLEAN: secrets 0 (only .env.example tracked; .env/.pem/.gcp gitignored). GEMINI_API_KEY server-side only (route.ts:42), no NEXT_PUBLIC on secrets, catch logs without leaking key. layout.tsx:88 dangerouslySetInnerHTML still benign static themeInitScript. No eval/exec/child_process/innerHTML in src.

COVERAGE GAP: Obsidian shared-vault tools unavailable again (5th run). NEXT SCAN: grep package.json for overrides; check if /api/categorize gained auth/rate-limit and if buildPrompt delimits user input.

content: Released v0.7.0 on 2026-05-29. v0.6.0 (trial-balance verifier) was already tagged at 8463c64 before this run. git log v0.6.0..HEAD = 2 feat, additive, none breaking -> MINOR per pre-1.0 0.x policy: 0ace17d (v3 upgrade: swappable data layer src/lib/data/, FIRST server API route POST /api/categorize calling real Gemini gemini-3.5-flash with heuristic fallback, ledger explorer UI w/ Zustand persist) + 44191ce (getFiscalPeriod date utility src/features/ledger/lib/period.ts, ADR-0006, pure fail-loud RangeError). Already on main (no branch merge). FULL GATE GREEN before tagging: tsc --noEmit clean, eslint clean (run as SEPARATE step from build per standing lesson ‚Äî Next 16 build does not run eslint), tsx --test 87/87, next build OK (8 routes incl /api/categorize dynamic). Release commit 8d43743 (bump package.json + CHANGELOG [Unreleased]->[0.7.0] folding in undocumented getFiscalPeriod + fixing test count to 87 + docs/releases/v0.7.0.md), annotated tag v0.7.0, pushed main+tag, published GitHub Release on xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper. CARRY-FORWARD non-blocking: (1) postcss GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 STILL OPEN (next@16.2.6 nests postcss@8.4.31), fix=package.json overrides {postcss:>=8.5.10}, no overrides block yet; (2) security findings on /api/categorize (cost-DoS unauth/unthrottled paid model; prompt injection via raw description) already routed to engineering by security persona ‚Äî documented in release notes, did not block. Obsidian shared-vault tools unavailable again.

ai-bookkeeper v0.7.0: v3 categorization wave self-documented README; period.ts + test counts were the gap
content: v0.7.0 (release commit 8d43743) shipped the 'smart categorization' wave v0.6.0 had EXCLUDED. Two feat commits: (1) 0ace17d v3 upgrade ‚Äî swappable data layer src/lib/data/, real Gemini POST /api/categorize Node route + heuristic fallback, CategorizePanel, ledger-explorer (search/filter/sort/CSV, Zustand-persisted prefs), dashboard async server components + loading/error boundaries. KEY: this dev commit ALSO updated README.md itself (Features/Tech-stack/Routes/Project-structure), so the big functional wave was self-documented ‚Äî unusual; normally README is fully my job. (2) 44191ce getFiscalPeriod pure date util (src/features/ledger/lib/period.ts), barrel-exported, 10 tests, did NOT touch README. Release Manager pre-authored CHANGELOG [0.7.0] + docs/releases/v0.7.0.md accurately (same pattern as v0.3.0-v0.6.0). Made 5 README doc-only edits: added `period` to the lib/ file-tree enumeration (STILL the highest-value recurring check ‚Äî even a README-touching dev commit missed it), corrected THREE stale test counts to 87 (were 77/77/42; suite 49->87), and added a getFiscalPeriod Features bullet. ADR caution: 44191ce/period.ts both cite [ADR-0006] but that's the home-link guard ADR ‚Äî fiscal-period has no ADR (next free 0007); did NOT propagate the mis-tag into README. Same mis-tag class as v0.6.0 trial-balance. LESSON: even when a dev commit self-edits the README, still run the lib/ enumeration + test-count checks ‚Äî those secondary fields are the ones dev commits forget.
